title: Fix buildah mount to display container names not ids
body:
Currently we are displaying containerid rather then containername,
which is unknown to users.  Users refer to the container names all
of the time, so we should show the mounted containers based on names.

This change eliminates the need for the truncate flag on buildah mount
as well, so this PR hides it.

Fix up the man page documentation to use container names and show
proper output.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Well it was triggered by the video showing people complaining about buildah bash completions.  Which I opened in a different PR, while doing that PR, I realized that `buildah unmount <tab><tab>` should list the mounted containers.  When I implemented that part, I saw that we were listing the IDs which was totally useless to users.  I want to umount alpine-working-container rather then 191939393
Hold off on hitting bors again.  I think we may have a problem that I'm investigating.

I was concerned this change might break a tutorial, but it does not, good to try kicking bors again.
LGTM

Bors why do you make me hate you?
bors retry
